title: Dryopteris oligophlebia
taxonomy: Dryopteris oligophlebia is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Dryopteris, which consists of several species of wood ferns. The genus Dryopteris is further classified under the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. This classification places Dryopteris oligophlebia within the kingdom Plantae. The species is native to Japan.

intro: Dryopteris oligophlebia, also known as the sparse-leaved wood fern, is a species of fern native to East Asia, characterized by its delicate fronds and sparse leaf arrangement. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce pain and alleviate symptoms of various diseases. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris oligophlebia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
